title: Labour law crash course in aid of Emmaüs
description: GEORGE NEWS - A labour law crash course will...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - A labour law crash course will be presented by local labour law expert Dr Lukas du Preez on Wednesday 22 May in George.


> The course is held for employer empowerment and in aid of the Emmaüs protected workshop for intellectually challenged people which is struggling financially due to lowered subsidies from the Department of Social Development, among others.


Du Preez's course covers the latest crucial aspects for employers in labour law related to effective disciplinary action and practical aspects such as suspension, desertion, harassment, intoxication, dishonesty, union interaction, dispute avoidance, strikes, etc.


> Video footage of an ideal disciplinary hearing and the actual dealing with a strike will be used.


This is a fast-moving, practical presentation covering the basic principles of labour law and latest developments, with the aim of preventing costly and time consuming disputes for employers.

Comprehensive workbooks and updated 'draft' employment contracts are included in the fee. Beverages and refreshments will be available.


> Du Preez is a well-known advocate with more than 40 years experience in labour law.


He consulted exclusively for management across industry throughout South Africa and acted. He consulted for large national and multi-national companies and many local employers in the Southern Cape in recent years.

He was also previously labour law advisor of the Afrikaanse Handelsinstituut (AHi) and holds a doctorate in labour law.

The cost of the course is R850.


> The course starts at 09:00 and concludes at 13:00 and the venue is Emmaüs, 6 Memoriam Street.


Bookings may be made with head of Emmaüs Elsable Badenhorst on 044 873 4196 or email to [email protected], or with Dr Lukas du Preez on 083 700 3134 or [email protected].

The bulk of the proceeds generated from this course will be exclusively allocated to the projects overseen by Emmaüs.

They really need the support.

Dr Lukas du Preez

Beautiful handwork items produced by Emmaüs workers.

Emmaüs workers create a variety of sturdy wooden toys and items that make lovely gifts.

We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
